subclassFeature.name: Shaded Soul
entries: Your soul is shrouded by the shadows, warped by their overwhelming presence. Your power came from a fragment of magic imbued upon you by the griping plane of shadows. your mind should've been shattered like many others who encountered such powers. But your Will is stronger.

entries.caption: Shaded Soul Sorcerer Quirks
rows: Your skin is reflectively pale, and you don't seem to be able to tan.
You can't seem to properly express emotions, instead the shadows warp and dance around you.
You can't see peoples faces, only their skeletons.
Your heart beats once per minute. This event sometimes surprises you.
Your arms and legs are covered in unwavering, and unmoving shadows.

entries.caption: Shaded Soul Origin Spells
rows: {@spell Dissonant Whispers}, {@spell Mind Sliver|TCE}, {@spell Pass without Trace}, {@spell Nondetection}

entries.name: A Shadow Beyond Sight
entries: You can flicker from the vision of your foes as your magic ensnares them. When you hit a creature with a spell attack from a sorcerer spell, or it fails its saving throw against a sorcerer spell, you can mark it with dark magic.
Until the end of your next turn, you become {@condition invisible} to that creature as long as you aren't within 10 feet of it.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier, you regain all uses after a rest.

entries.name: Eyes of the Dark
entries: You can see normally in the dark and dim conditions with a range of 120 feet, and you can see through all magical darkness. Additionally you can expend 3 sorcery points to see through others magical darkness for 1 hour.
You learn the {@spell darkness} spell, which doesn't count against your number of sorcerer spells known. Which you can cast by spending 3 sorcery points.

subclassFeature.name: Cloak of Enveloping Shade
entries: You gain resistance to psychic damage.
As a {@variantrule bonus action|xphb}, you can spend 3 sorcery points to shroud yourself in protective shadow for 1 minute. This effect does not require {@variantrule concentration|xphb}.
While the cloak is active:

items: Your walking speed increases by 10 feet.
You have {@variantrule advantage|xphb} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made in dim light or darkness.

entries: You can extend the duration by 1 minute by spending 2 additional sorcery points, up to a maximum duration of 3 minutes total.

entries.name: Shadow Walk
entries: While you are in dim light or darkness, you can use a {@variantrule bonus action|xphb} to teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in dim light or darkness.
After teleporting, you have {@variantrule advantage|xphb} on the first attack roll you make before the end of the turn.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule long rest|xphb}.

subclassFeature.name: Eclipse Dome
entries: As an action, you can spend 5 sorcery points to create a 20-foot-radius sphere of magical darkness centered on a point you can see within 60 feet. The dome lasts for 1 minute.
You know the location of all creatures within the dome while you are inside it, though you do not learn their conditions.
When you create the dome, you gain temporary hit points equal to your sorcerer level.
You choose any number of creatures you can see when the dome is created. Those creatures can see normally within the dome.

All other creatures treat the area as {@quickref heavily obscured|xphb|3|0|heavily obscured} unless they possess {@sense blindsight} or {@sense truesight}. Creatures that rely on {@sense darkvision} do not see through this darkness.
This feature can be used once per {@variantrule long rest|xphb}. You can use it again by spending 6 sorcery points.

subclassFeature.name: Umbral Wave
entries: You gain immunity to psychic damage.
While in dim light or darkness, you can take the {@action Hide|xphb} action even if you are only lightly obscured.
As an action, you can spend 5 sorcery points to unleash a wave of shadow in a 30-foot cone. Creatures of your choice in the area must make an Intelligence saving throw against your spell save DC.
On a failed save:

items: The creature is {@condition blinded} until the end of your next turn.
Until that blindness ends, once per turn when you move at least 10 feet, you can teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of one creature {@condition blinded} by this feature.

entries: On a successful save:

items: The creature is not {@condition blinded} but has {@variantrule disadvantage|xphb} on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

entries: You can use this feature once per {@variantrule long rest|xphb}. You can use it again by spending 6 sorcery points.

subclassFeature.name: Deadly Trickster
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you gain advantage in Performance and Deception checks. Additionally, as an action, you can cast Disguise Self on a Puppet and speak or act through it. When you cast Disguise Self with this ability, you can create a perfect copy of a person that you have seen and studied for at least 1 minute. The Puppet imitates the voice and physical behaviors of that person perfectly. While disguised in this way, the puppet gains advantage on all checks made to influence an ally of the person they are imitating.

subclassFeature.name: Charming Voice
entries: Starting at 7th level, your puppet can attempt to charm a creature that can hear it and is within 60ft of the puppet. Only one creature can be targeted by a puppet at a time. The target must pass a Charisma saving throw or be charmed for 1 minute. While charmed in this way, the creature is friendly to you, your puppets, and your allies. While charmed in this way, they can follow basic commands, such as 'Go over there' or 'Attack that creature.' They are not, however, hostile to their allies. Any order that would inflict harm on the creature's allies or itself causes the effect of Charming Voice to immediately end. While the puppet is disguised as an ally of the target due to the effects of Deadly Trickster, they make the saving throw at disadvantage.

subclassFeature.name: Illusory Mimicry
entries: Beginning at 11th level, when a creature makes an attack roll against you, as a natural reaction to danger, you can dissipate an illusory duplicate of yourself by expending one of your spell slots. The attack automatically misses, as if you were never there. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Artisan Killer
entries: Starting at 17th level, a Puppet under the effects of Deadly Trickster may attack twice per round while it is disguised. When attacking from a disguised or in a hidden position in this form, the puppet can make both attacks before being revealed. It gains advantage on the attack roll and does bonus damage equal to your Charisma modifier. The illusion ends for a creature when a puppet attacks it, though it does not end for other creatures unless they pass a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC. If a creature sees the puppet attack one of its allies while in its disguised form, it gains advantage on the check.

subclassFeature.name: Puppet Ledgerdomain
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can temporarily dismiss your Puppets from the world. They disappear into a pocket dimension where they await your summon. As an action, while they are temporarily dismissed, you can cause them to re-appear in any unoccupied space within 30 feet of you, with the effects of Dying Effigy.

subclassFeature.name: Raggedy Doll
entries: Starting at 7th level, your puppets count as one size larger when determining their carrying capacity and the weight they can push, drag, or lift. When they take damage, you can use your reaction to roll a d12. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled, and reduce the damage by that total.

subclassFeature.name: Death Marionette
entries: Starting at 11th level, your Puppets can move stealthily at a normal pace. They have advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn in the combat yet. In addition, any hit they score against a creature that is surprised is a critical hit.

subclassFeature.name: Sewn Chaos
entries: At 17th level, your Puppets gain the ability to distract your enemies. As a bonus action on your turn, you can designate a creature within 10 feet of your puppet. Doing so grants allies advantage on attack rolls against that target. The first time a creature is hit with this effect, it must pass an Intelligence saving throw against your spell save DC or be Surprised until the end of its next turn. When the enemy is Surprised in this way, any hit scored against them is a critical hit. When you use this feature, the puppet cannot move nor act, and can only serve as a distraction.

item.name: Miner's Tools
entries: Miner's Tools include a heavy pick, a shovel, a hammer, chisels, and a stiff brush used to excavate earth and identify ore.

items.name: Utilize
items.entry: Chip off a stone sample ({@dc 10}), Determine tunnel stability ({@dc 15}), Extract a gem or crystal intact ({@dc 15}), Create a small defensive earthwork ({@dc 15}).

entries: If you have proficiency with a tool, add your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check too.

You have toiled in the sunless depths where the veil between the Material Plane and the Shadowfell is dangerously thin. Your hands are callous and stained with coal and dark crystal dust, and your lungs have grown accustomed to air that tastes of stale magic. While others fear the dark, you have learned to navigate it.

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Miner's Pick|PHB}, {@item Shovel|XPHB}, {@item Dungeoneer's Pack|XPHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 10 GP; or (B) 50 GP

entries: The machinery of the kingdom runs on ink, stamps, and the occasional silenced witness. You were once a cog in this corrupt engine, falsifying reports to cover up the horrors of the mines. Whether you fled due to guilt or became a liability, you carry the secrets of the state.

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB}, {@item Fine Clothes|XPHB}, {@item Ink|XPHB}, {@item Ink Pen|XPHB}, {@item Paper|XPHB}, {@item Map or Scroll Case|XPHB}, 20 GP; or (B) 50 GP

entries: You wore the badge of the crown, sworn to uphold order in a kingdom sliding into chaos. Now, disillusioned by the corruption you served, you walk a fine line between the lawman you were trained to be and the vigilante the world needs.

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Manacles|XPHB}, {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB}, {@item Signal Whistle|XPHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, {@item Bullseye Lantern|XPHB}, {@item Oil|XPHB}, 10 GP; or (B) 50 GP

entries: Where others see terrifying monsters and creeping gloom, you see a fascinating ecosystem waiting to be cataloged. You have dedicated your life to studying the bleed of the Shadowfell, risking your sanity to understand the metaphysics of darkness.

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}, {@item Scholar's Pack|XPHB}, {@item Robe|XPHB}, 15 GP; or (B) 50 GP

entries: Born in the gutters under the shadow of tyrannical spires, you are the voice of the voiceless. Whether organizing strikes, spreading propaganda, or sabotaging supply lines, you fight for a dawn that seems impossible to reach.

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}, {@item Crowbar|XPHB}, {@item Burglar's Pack|XPHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 5 GP; or (B) 50 GP

entries: In a crumbling kingdom, morality is a luxury you cannot afford. You operate in the gray markets, dealing in illicit goods. You understand the value of everything and know exactly whose palms to grease to get what you want.

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB}, {@item Dice Set|XPHB}, {@item Fine Clothes|XPHB}, {@item Dagger|XPHB}, 15 GP; or (B) 50 GP

entries: Your name was once spoken with reverence, but now it is whispered as a cautionary tale. Cast out for political failure, you retain your education and bearing. You carry the weight of a fallen house, seeking to reclaim your honor.

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Dragonchess Set|XPHB}, {@item Fine Clothes|XPHB}, {@item Signet Ring|PHB}, {@item Perfume|XPHB}, {@item Steel Mirror|PHB}, 25 GP; or (B) 50 GP

entries: Identity is fluid, and truth is a commodity. You are a master of blending in. You have sold secrets to both the crown and the resistance, surviving by making yourself indispensable—and invisible.

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Forgery Kit|XPHB}, {@item Hooded Lantern|XPHB}, {@item Oil|XPHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, {@item Chalk (1 piece)|PHB}, 15 GP; or (B) 50 GP

entries: You swore oaths to protect the weak, but the Shadowfell's encroachment shattered those ideals. Having forsaken your title, you are now a warrior without a master, a blade seeking a new purpose in a dying world.

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}, {@item Whetstone|PHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, {@item Explorer's Pack|XPHB}, {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB}, 10 GP; or (B) 50 GP

entries: The Shadowfell did not just touch your life; it seeped into your blood. Raised in the proximity of dark rifts, you possess an innate connection to the gloom. Normal society shuns you, but you find comfort in the dark places.

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB}, {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}, {@item Common Clothes|PHB}, {@item Blanket|XPHB}, {@item Tinderbox|XPHB}, 10 GP; or (B) 50 GP

feat.name: Deep Delver
entries: You have adapted to the crushing dark of the mines. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You gain {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have darkvision, its range increases by 30 feet.

entries.name: Subterranean Survival
entries: You have {@variantrule advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Wisdom} ({@skill Survival}) checks made while underground or in the Shadowfell.

entries.name: Tremorsense
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can gain {@sense tremorsense} with a range of 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

feat.name: Forger's Insight
entries: Your experience with bureaucracy allows you to manipulate official records. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Tool Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with the {@item forgery kit|XPHB}.

entries.name: Mimicry
entries: If you spend at least 1 minute observing a creature's handwriting, you can mimic it perfectly.

entries.name: Bureaucratic Deception
entries: When you make a {@skill Deception} check involving written documents or bureaucratic procedures, you can use your {@skill Intelligence} modifier instead of your {@skill Charisma} modifier.

feat.name: Badge of Authority
entries: You still carry the aura of the law, however tarnished. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Commanding Voice
entries: You learn the {@spell Command} spell. You can cast it once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Intimidating Presence
entries: You have {@variantrule advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Intimidation} checks directed at commoners or low-ranking soldiers affiliated with the kingdom.

feat.name: Shadow Scholar
entries: Your research into forbidden lore grants you minor magical abilities. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Cantrip
entries: You learn one cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Wizard spell list|spells|class=Wizard|level=0|school=N;I}. {@skill Intelligence} is your spellcasting ability for it.

entries.name: Detect Shadow
entries: You can cast {@spell Detect Magic} once without expending a spell slot. When cast this way, the spell specifically highlights auras originating from the Shadowfell with a deep purple hue. You regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Urban Guerrilla
entries: You are adept at fighting and moving within the city's underbelly. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Street Movement
entries: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement if you are in an urban environment (such as sewers, alleys, or ruins).

entries.name: Defiant Spirit
entries: When you fail a saving throw against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}, you can use your reaction to reroll the save. You must use the new roll. You can use this feature once per {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Black Market Contacts
entries: You know how to navigate the criminal underworld. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Thieves' Cant
entries: You learn {@language Thieves' Cant}.

entries.name: Underworld Dealings
entries: While in a city or settlement, you can find fences willing to buy stolen non-magical goods at full value, or merchants willing to sell poisons and potions at a 20 percent discount.

feat.name: Noble Presence
entries: Your upbringing demands respect, inspiring those around you. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Inspiring Magic
entries: You learn the {@spell Heroism} spell. You can cast it once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Social Influence
entries: You have a pool of 3 social points. When you make a {@skill Persuasion} check, you can spend 1 social point to gain {@variantrule advantage|XPHB} on the roll. You regain all expended points when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Face Dancer
entries: You are a master of disguise and infiltration. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Master of Disguise
entries: You can cast {@spell Disguise Self} once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to do so when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Unreadable
entries: Creatures have {@variantrule disadvantage|XPHB} on {@skill Insight} checks made to determine your true identity or intentions while you are disguised.

feat.name: Oathbreaker's Strike
entries: You channel the pain of your broken vows into your strikes. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Heavy Armor Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with Heavy Armor.

entries.name: Necrotic Strike
entries: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage to the target.

feat.name: Child of the Gloom
entries: The shadow energy infused in your blood protects you. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Shadow Resilience
entries: You have resistance to necrotic damage.

entries.name: Gloom Thaumaturgy
entries: You learn the {@spell Thaumaturgy} cantrip. When you cast it, the sensory effects manifest as shadowy distortions (e.g., dimming lights, whispered echoes).

weapons: martial weapons that have the finesse or light property

default: (a) A weapon with the Finesse property or (b) Any simple weapon or (c) Any crossbow
A Necromancer's Journal to Puppet Crafting

classFeature.name: Personal Puppet
entries: You can possess two puppets at any given time. At 7th level, you can hold up to three, and at 15th level you can hold up to four. You can create or raise a puppet that has a CR equal to or less than your CR Limit. Furthermore, the total CR of all puppets added together must be equal to or less than your CR Limit.

When you select this class, you begin with a Child's Marionette Doll, which acts as your personal puppet. It may be any one of the five puppet options. This puppet, as well as all puppets you control or create, have the following traits:

items: It has immunity to poison damage and the poisoned condition.
It has Darkvision out to a range of 60ft.
It can speak in your voice, or in a monotone, in languages you know.
It shares your initiative and goes directly after you.
Once given an order, the Puppet continues to follow it until its task is complete.
It adds your proficiency bonus to their attack rolls and saving throws, and use your Spell Save DC.

If you are incapacitated, absent, or your psychic string breaks, the puppet is unable to act on its own. You may use your reaction upon the string breaking to reestablish a connection with them or choose to do so action on your turn.
It doesn't need to sleep and doesn't suffer the effects of exhaustion due to lack of rest, and magic can't put it to sleep.

entries: Starting at 5th level, this puppet, as well as all other puppets you create or take control over, adds half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) to their damage rolls. At 10th level, your puppets add your full proficiency bonus to your damage rolls.
Puppets you create have a number of hit die equal to your Puppeteer level, and you add your proficiency bonus to each of their stats.

You may create more puppets to serve you or repair puppets currently under your service. In order to create a puppet, you must make a DC 12 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check with a set of either: Tinker's tools, Leatherworker's tools, or Weaver's tools. It takes 10 minutes to create a puppet.

Not only can you imbue creatures with the foul mimicry of life, but you may also attempt to bring back those who have fallen. As an action, you can force the dead creature's spirit to roll a Charisma saving throw against your DC. If it fails, it becomes your puppet after 7 - your proficiency bonus amount of rounds of reanimating itself.

classFeature.name: Psychic Strings
entries: Thin psychic strings shoot forth from your outstretched fingertips, allowing you to control your puppets while they are within a number of feet equal to 30 times your Puppeteer level. If a puppet exceeds this range, they become inactive until you are within range, at which point your psychic strings reconnect with the puppet (no action required).

classFeature.name: Command Puppet
entries: On each of your turns, you can telepathically command any Puppet you make or take control over with this feature within 60 feet of you (if you control multiple Puppets, you can command any or all of them at the same time, issuing the same or different commands to each one). You may control one puppet when you select this class. The number of puppets you can control at one time increases to two at level 5, three at level 10, and four at level 15.

Puppets under your control obey your commands to the best of their ability. You may fiddle with their strings, controlling them. Doing so allows you to choose the life-sized Puppet's actions and bonus actions.

classFeature.name: Dying Effigy
entries: You learn to pull the threads of magic laced in the puppet to bring it forward and take blows for you. When you would take damage, you can use your reaction to have a puppet take the damage instead if it is within 60 feet of you and line of sight. The puppet then appears in an unoccupied space within 5ft of you. This ability can be used a number of times equal to Charisma modifier.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: The Puppeteer Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a Long Rest.

{@b Prepared Spells of Level 1+.} You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you, choosing from the Bard spell list. To start, choose two level 1 Bard spells. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Puppeteer levels.
{@b Spellcasting Ability.} Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Puppeteer spells.
{@b Spellcasting Focus.} You can use an animate puppet as a Spellcasting Focus for your Puppeteer spells.

classFeature.name: Soul Harvest
entries: At 2nd level, you gain the ability to reap life energy from creatures you or your puppet's kill. Once per turn when you or your puppets kill one or more creatures with a spell of 1st level or higher, you regain hit points equal to twice the spell's level, or three times its level if the spell belongs to the School of Necromancy. You don't gain this benefit for killing constructs or undead.

classFeature.name: Art of Puppetry
entries: Beginning at 3rd level, Puppeteers begin to study their craft extensively, bringing forth various Arts of Puppetry. They offer the practitioner the one thing that binds their craft together. You learn an Art of Puppetry feature at 3rd, 6th, 8th & 17th levels.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Starting at 5th level, you can attack twice, rather than once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. Your puppets also gain this feature, though it does not stack with existing Extra Attack or Multi Attack features.

classFeature.name: Weaken Will
entries: Starting at 6th level, when you attempt to bring a creature back to life, it has disadvantage on its saving throw.

classFeature.name: Charming Continuance
entries: Starting at 7th level, if damage reduces a puppet to 0 hit points, it must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 5+the damage taken, unless the damage is radiant or from a critical hit. On a success, the puppet drops to 1 hit point instead. Furthermore, puppets add your Charisma modifier to their death saving throws.

classFeature.name: Hades' Blessing
entries: Starting at 9th both you and your puppets add your Charisma modifier to initiative rolls. Furthermore, you and your puppets attacks become magical for the purpose of overcoming damage resistances and immunity.

classFeature.name: Razor Strings
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can now create incredibly sharp strings designed for slowing down and injuring your enemies. These strings act as both a hazard and difficult terrain. Creatures you designate to see the string can avoid its effects, thought the area occupied by your strings still count as difficult terrain for them. Any creature unaware of these strings must pass a Constitution saving throw against your Spell Save DC when it enters a space they occupy or take damage equal to your level. A creature repeats this saving throw each time it enters a new 5ft space your Razor Strings occupy, though it makes both Perception checks to perceive the string and Constitution savings throws to avoid taking damage at advantage.

These strings last for 1 hour, until destroyed, or until you dismiss them (no action required). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain all uses on a long rest.

classFeature.name: Contingency
entries: Starting at 13th level, your dealings with the underworld has further fueled you and your puppets' powers. Both you and your puppets are immune to disease, gain resistance to necrotic damage, and your hit point maximums cannot be reduced.

classFeature.name: Parasitic Strings
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can make a ranged spell attack against one Large or smaller creature within 120ft of you that you can see. On a hit, a string is attached to that creature, and it then becomes your puppet. While this creature is your puppet, you control its actions. At the beginning of each of its turns, it must make a Charisma saving throw against your DC. On a success, the creature breaks free from your grasp. This spell ends after a Charisma modifier amount of rounds. This ability can be used only once every 24 hours on the same creature. You may use this ability three times per long rest. You regain one use on short rest, and all uses on a long rest.

classFeature.name: Tied Lives
entries: Starting at 15th level, as an action, you may attach a string between two creatures or one object and one creature within 120ft of you that you can see. You may target yourself with this ability. This string remains for 1 minute, until it is destroyed, or you dismiss them (no action required).

While this string attached, you gain access to bonus actions such as Energy Flow, Shared Vitality, Hook, Line, and Anchor, and Pain Slit (refer to full class description for specifics). You may use this feature twice before needing a long rest.

classFeature.name: Snapped
entries: Starting at 18th level, as an action, you can force a puppet to lose one fifth of its maximum hit points and cause an effect to occur, based on the model (Duelist, Magic Attacker, Puppet of All Trades, First Aid Puppet, or Broken Puppet). This feature can be used twice per long rest.

classFeature.name: Underworld's Fury
entries: Starting at 18th level, as an action, you can choose one creature you raised from death to lose one-fifth of its maximum hit points and gain the following benefits: Fire immunity for 1 minute, advantage on all conditions for 1 minute, and the benefit of the Haste spell, no Concentration required, for 1 minute. A puppet whose hit point maximum is reduced in this way can only recover them on a long rest. This feature can be used twice per long rest.

classFeature.name: Follow the Horde
entries: Starting at 19th level, your puppets are able to help expand your legion. As an action on their turn, they can designate any creature it can see within 30ft of itself to make a Constitution saving throw. On a fail, the creature is beguiled by the dark, infectious energy seeping from the puppet, and is charmed for one minute. While charmed in this way, it is under the effects of Dominate Monster. This effect requires both you and the puppet to concentrate on it and ends if either of you breaks your concentration. You can only have one creature under this effect at one time.

classFeature.name: Unhinged Souls
entries: At 20th level, as an action, you are able to release control of your puppets for a brief moment of time. When you do so, the following effects occur: The puppet's hit point maximum permanently decreases to half its current maximum. Each puppet is treated with the effects of Dispel Magic and Counterspell as a 9th level spell. Each puppet gains resistance to all damage and immunity to all conditions, and Magic Resistance.

Each puppet can take a second action, gains a bonus to AC equal to your charisma modifier, and gains a bonus to speed equal to your current speed. Puppets cannot concentrate during this time and attack the closest thing in the vicinity to itself, including you.

As part of this action, you may draw a 5ft circle in a location you can see. A puppet under the effects of Unhinged will not attack a creature within the space. This effect lasts for 1d4 + 1 rounds, and puppets cannot act for the same amount of turns afterward.
Additionally, you may cast Magic Jar and Soul Cage once each per long rest. This feature can be used once per long rest.
